text: Good code is not just about working – it’s about being readable and easy to maintain. One of the most important habits is following consistent naming conventions. For example, using clear names for variables and functions helps anyone understand what the code does without reading every line. Another key idea is breaking code into small functions, each with a single purpose. This makes your program more organised and easier to test or change later. Clean code saves time in the long run, especially when you or others need to update it. In short, writing clean code is a sign of a thoughtful and professional developer.
